text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village nestled amidst chestnut trees, there stood an old oak tree. The animals didn side eof it every autumn, whispering tales of old.

Our favorite squirrel, Orion, loved those stories. Every night before bedtime.

One autumn night, Orion nestled into a walnut shell. He listened closely. They talked about golden eagles soaring overhead, fierce protectors.

Orion dreamed of courage. Then morning stars winked at him.

Orion didn't dream again that night.

The next day, he braved oak leaf shadows. He saw an eagle. It flew away swiftly.

OrION!

That night, Orion couldn't sleep.

Stars whispered tales.

Orion became an eagle. Soaring proudly.

Winter thawed birds.

Orion flew south.

Southern skies glittered.

Orion saw his favorite oak tree.

Oh.

He fell-back into leaves.

Orion giggled.

That's my favorite story.

He cherished oak tree dreams forever.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|>
